next_experiment: Characterize the null exceptional code set and prove that every natural valuation code leaves it while exceeding its affine descent threshold.
claim_boundary: No conjecture proof and no certified conjecture counterexample.
proof_boundary: The natural positive odd integers form a countable dense Haar-null set, so they could lie inside the exceptional null set. Moreover a slope below one does not by itself beat the positive affine term for a given integer. This theorem is not a pointwise Collatz descent proof.
